meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no version control (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve No Version Control
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement user onboarding to mitigate the risks of no version control. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Without version control, your code is a house of cards. By implementing a robust user onboarding flow, you create a controlled entry point that validates user input and state, effectively shielding your application from corrupted data. This architecture allows you to track progress and recover state even without complex Git history.
content.intro:
The midnight oil is burning, your screen is a blur of syntax, and suddenly, a single rogue keystroke wipes out your entire progress. You have no Git history, no branches, and no safety net. This is the 'No Version Control' nightmare that haunts every student developer building their first SaaS project.

But here is the secret: you don't need a complex CI/CD pipeline to protect your work. By building a disciplined user onboarding flow, you create a system that enforces state management and data integrity from the moment a user signs up. You aren't just building a feature; you are building a professional guardrail for your application.

steps.title: Define the Onboarding Schema
steps.description: Establish a rigid JSON structure for user data to ensure every onboarding step is validated before it hits your database.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a TypeScript interface for a 3-step user onboarding flow including name, project goal, and technical experience level. Add Zod validation schema.

steps.title: Implement State Persistence
steps.description: Use local storage as a temporary buffer to ensure that if a session crashes, the user's progress is not lost.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a React hook useOnboardingState that syncs form input to localStorage and provides a reset function for testing.

steps.title: Build the Progress Guardrail
steps.description: Add a visual step indicator that prevents users from proceeding until the current step data is validated.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a ProgressStepper component that disables the 'Next' button if the current Zod validation fails.

steps.title: Automate Data Sanitization
steps.description: Prevent malformed data from entering your app by sanitizing inputs during the onboarding flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add a middleware function to sanitize onboarding input strings to prevent XSS and malformed data entries.

steps.title: Finalize and Deploy
steps.description: Package the onboarding flow into a deployable module that integrates with your main application entry point.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the onboarding components into a single 'OnboardingModule' that can be lazily loaded in the main App.tsx.

pro_tips: Always treat your onboarding data as immutable; never mutate the original state object directly.
Use 'feature flags' in your onboarding flow to A/B test which user questions lead to higher retention.
Implement a 'Save Progress' button even on simple forms to build the habit of frequent data commits.
Use console.table() during development to visualize state changes in real-time within your browser.
Document your onboarding logic in a README.md file; it is the first thing recruiters look for in your repo.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is live, start implementing automated unit tests for each step to ensure your logic remains intact as you add new features.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting 'no version control' hold back your potential. You have the tools, the logic, and the blueprint to build something world-class today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
